mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly payroll growth above 100k signals robust labor market conditions that boost consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in periods with PAYEMS > 100k are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.05 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in both GDP growth (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) signal weakening demand, creating a negative feedback loop that depresses S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in periods with declining GDPC1 and HOUST are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or interaction effect size < 0.15.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above 1.5% signals strengthening supply-side capacity, which reduces inflationary pressures and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in INDPRO > 1.5% periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 3% (CPIAUCSL) during a monetary tightening cycle forces central banks to maintain high rates, creating headwinds for equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in high-CPI/high-FEDFUNDS regimes are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or interaction effect size < 0.20.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in both consumer confidence (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates deteriorating demand conditions, creating a negative feedback loop that depresses S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in periods with declining RSAFS and HOUST are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or interaction effect size < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in unemployment (UNRATE) below 4% signals tight labor market conditions that drive wage inflation and corporate cost pressures, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in low-UNRATE periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above 1.0% signals improving supply-side efficiency, which reduces input costs and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in INDPRO > 1.0% periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.04 over 2-year rolling windows.
